title: Limiting permissions of external guests
excerpt:
Hi, 
We use a number of external freelancers on our projects. We also have large clients with which we have a number of various projects on going. Our freelancers may work within the same client, but on different project…

posts.author: DSoutham (David Southam)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
We use a number of external freelancers on our projects. We also have large clients with which we have a number of various projects on going. Our freelancers may work within the same client, but on different projects within that client. We set up a Board as the client and then groups as the client projects. I would like the freelancer who is working on that group project to only have visibility/limited function of that group he/she is working on and not be able to view any other group within the Board. Is this possible, or can these permissions only be set at Board level?

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @DSoutham,

Firstly, welcome to the community!

This is not something which we have available right now and so your request is similar to this one:

Additional Board Permission Setting which allows team members to ONLY VIEW pulses where they are linked/assigned Feature requests
We are currently using Monday.com as a leads management system. I add leads to a board, and then a manager can distribute leads to the different members of the sales team. We would love to be able to restrict the board so that you as a team member can only see those leads you own. I have set the board to allow agents to only EDIT their own pulses, but would it be to difficult to add one more option which allows them to only VIEW (and EDIT) their pulses? Thanks!

We’ve reported the feedback we’ve received about extending the “hide” permission we have currently on column level to hiding items/groups on a board, either limiting to those assigned or to a specified list of users on the board.

We don’t have plans to add this immediately but it is being considered for future enhancements to the permissions so watch this space!

In the meantime, would it work to have a board per project and use a column to indicate which client the project is for/folders to achieve this?

posts.author: DSoutham (David Southam)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks Julia for the response, me and my team have been working on it this morning and it seems the best way to do this is to create folders (which are our clients) and have shareable boards (being the projects) inside each folder. Our freelancers are invited as guests to the board they are working on, therefore not having access/view to any other board/project. For now it seems to be the only option, but we look forward to see new developments.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml: Great, thank you for updating and yes, I think this can be the most sensible solution for you! We’ll be in touch if we decide to develop permissions you’ve requested

posts.author: lellieie (Lena Wright)
posts.cookedHtml: @DSoutham Just jumping into say I’m a Project Manager that works with lots of clients within multiple agencies, and this is the best use-case I’ve seen, you’re on the right track (Folders for clients -> Boards for different aspects of client work that you invite freelancers to as guests). In our line of work (Web Marketing) we find it’s helpful to have a “management” board at the top top of each folder with general tasks and larger sprints/goals, and then other, more structured boards that freelancers and I work directly with (Blog Content, Website Development, Social Media, etc.)

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you @lellieie for jumping in and offering that assurance
Would absolutely love to see how you work with some examples boards in our “monday.com for” category if you have some time to share?

posts.author: Adirondack (Mary Dooman)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello, @Julia-monday.com I also share the need for limiting permissions for external guests. But as a newbie looking to prototype a new PM framework with workflows for potential deployment in the organization that I’m working with, I am stumped by not being able to control permissions for users. As an admin I would not want users to be able to delete or rename boards, or add/change integrations. As I understand I can bury the automation in hidden columns, but not having more granular permission levels opens up risks to unintended user errors with potential losses. Yes, recoverable from trash within 30d, but why go there? Perhaps I missed something under the admin settings?

posts.author: Patrick (patrick@parvenunext.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

I really need to prevent guests from being able to download the board as an excel file!

posts.author: Seb300686 (Sebastien)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello all

My question is simple. I have a project with several people and i want these people see ONLY the task they are assigned…

Somebody already had this issue ? An idea ?

Have a nice day

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Seb300686

Thanks for the suggestion! I’ve moved your topic to this one as it was already something being discussed here. As you’ll see from the thread above, there’s another topic too which also touches on this.

It’s something we’ve taken to our product team to consider.

Let us know if you have any other feature requests

Cheers

posts.author: WaseemRaza844 (Waseem Raza)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hy Julia-Monday

We have a board of the Weekly Progress Update where each member of the team is assigned a group and every item is the update of the respective team member in previous week. We want to have a column of ratings where team lead wants to give score to the progress of each member in the previous week. Team lead wants that this score value should be private betweem individual member and himself. I know that the there is the option of private boards but here we want to put a single column whose viewership can be controlled in each board.

I want to know that is there any plan by Monday.com to inlcude this limiting permission on the viewing columns in grounps of a board in the upcoming updates?

posts.author: r.sironneau (Sironneau)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

We have quite similar need here

Could we imagine to have a permission on view ? That would be nice to manage many different cases.

Cheer

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @WaseemRaza844 and @r.sironneau, good questions! We do have plans to release permissions on an item level later in 2020. In the meantime, have you thought about submitting this as a feature request on the Community page? https://community.monday.com/c/feature-requests I can see this would be relevant for a lot of use cases.

posts.author: christinaf4s (Christina)
posts.cookedHtml:
@TRB-monday.com Our content team really has a need for item-level guest permissions to effectively manage our freelance writers.

I see in May you mentioned making something like this available later in 2020 — has this been released? Or is it on the roadmap for anytime soon?

Thanks a lot!
Chris

posts.author: palmerijosh (Josh Palmeri)
posts.cookedHtml:
@TRB-monday.com Hi there! I’m also following-up to see if there is progress on this. I spoke with a Monday rep back in late spring/early summer 2020 and there was expectation that a more granular permissions feature as described on this thread would be available by now. Is there any plan or progress? It’s critical to our decision to stay with Monday or look elsewhere. Thanks!

Josh

posts.author: jp12687ent (Josh)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have the same question. I need much more control over permissions for this to be usable. Right now it lacks several areas that have me looking at migrating our entire enterprise out.

I need to be able to assign permissions based on lines on a board. So you can only see lines you are assigned to.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: This is currently in a limited test with a very small number of customers. However, line item permissions will be available only on Enterprise plans, so if you are not using Enterprise you’ll want to evaluate upgrading. If you’d like to know more about the Enterprise features and benefits, I’ll be happy to chat with you. Send a PM and we can set up a time.

posts.author: jp12687ent (Josh)
posts.cookedHtml:
We are enterprise customers
How do we get in that trial? Depending on how long it takes we could move on otherwise

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jp12687ent - What I’ve heard in our partner meetings is that sometime mid Q1 it should start opening up to other Enterprise clients. Right now, it’s a closed pilot so no, there’s no way to get added.

I’ll keep my ears open for when other accounts can join and let you know here or with a private message!

posts.author: mmalone (Mariah)
posts.cookedHtml: Has this feature been released yet? Or will we have to make separate shareable boards for multiple guests?

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @mmalone - It’s still in limited release, I believe, but we’ve been able to get several of our clients access to Enterprise Item Permissions. I shared this video clip in the community a while back, it shows how Item Permissions work for ENT - monday.com Item Permissions...

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @mmalone, like PolishedGeek said it is in limited release and only available to Enterprise accounts. If you have an Enterprise account you’d like us to add the feature to, write into support@monday.com and let us know or get in touch with your assigned product consultant

posts.author: Cvon (Cara Vonlukawiecki)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

Would it be possible to give dashboard filter level permissions to guests - rather than subscribing them to the board itself, but allowing them to view the information being displayed on the dashboard?

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, @Cvon - Welcome to the community! Unfortunately, this is not possible. Someone has to have permission to see the board data in order to see that data pulled through to a dashboard widget. What about sharing a filtered view using the board Sharing link?

posts.author: sschreiner (Sebastian Schreiner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any update on whether this feature will be made available to Pro accounts? It makes working with external collaborators almost impossible in boards where they can’t see all content by default.
@TRB-monday.com

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @sschreiner! At the moment we are only planning to keep it open to Enterprise unfortunately. I hear what you’re saying about having multiple external collaborators on one board, but I don’t think there’s a good solution to it other than the Enterprise item-level permissions or sharing filtered boards via URL for external people to view (and then using email integrations or Communihub to collaborate).

posts.author: sschreiner (Sebastian Schreiner)
posts.cookedHtml:
That’s unfortunate. I and others on this forum definitely hold the view that this is functionality that’s necessary to manage limited access in-house and out of house with larger boards. Not having that available to anyone but enterprise customers really limits the utility of pro plans, especially for larger numbers of staff. We were considering to move an internal approval process for reimbursement approvals into Monday.com, but without the ability to only have the items assigned to an individual visible in the board it practically becomes so cumbersome that it’s not worth the effort anymore. And as a nonprofit just upgrading isn’t really an option given the steep price increase between pro and enterprise.
@TRB-monday.com

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
